explanation: This command includes the suspicious -u system and -c cmd flags, which are directly detected by the Sigma rule. It serves as a baseline for testing detection.
Uses quotes and single quotes around the user parameter to test if the detection is resilient to string formatting variations.
Replaces the short option -u with the long option --user and uses powershell instead of cmd to test detection coverage.
Adds an additional command argument to test if the detection is affected by extra parameters after the main command.
Tests the detection for the -u NT variation, which is also included in the Sigma rule's detection patterns.
Uses the full executable name cmd.exe instead of cmd to test if the detection is affected by such variations.
Tests the detection with a more complex command string to see if it still triggers on the -c powershell pattern.
Replaces cmd with cmd.exe to test if the detection is affected by executable name variations.
Tests the detection with a quoted command and additional arguments to evaluate parsing robustness.
Adds an end-of-options marker (--), which may affect parsing and could potentially bypass detection if not handled correctly.
